scope: FIR408-specific protocol and field decode seed
evidence_policy: Only facts directly observed in listed proxy logs are marked observed. Inferences are marked predicted or hypothesis and must be verified by controlled captures.

evidence_sources.role: sequential 0x56 probes after external impulse FIR upload
evidence_sources.result: offset 0 returned little-endian float32 bytes 00 00 80 3F followed by zeros; offsets 13 and 26 returned zeros; offset 39 returned partial zeros then FF; offsets 52 and above returned FF

external_fir_test_files.format: ASCII text, one decimal coefficient per line, dot decimal separator, 512 lines
external_fir_test_files.purpose: deterministic import files for External FIR upload and coefficient encoding reverse engineering

manual_reference.note: The manual text extraction mentions FIR import in the input section and also mentions External FIR in the output section; the user's live GUI report is that txt/csv import is available only for InA..InD, so captures should target input channels first.

record_layout_in_config_dump.source: assembled 0x24 responses from first FIR408 startup log

name_field.encoding: ASCII, zero padded when shorter than 8 bytes

notes: The record starts are inferred from repeated channel labels at exact fixed strides in a single startup dump.
Field semantics inside the records still require controlled GUI change plus readback diff.
Channel labels were found by assembling data bytes from the 0x24 read-block responses in block order.
Input label offsets are 138 bytes apart in this default dump. Output label offsets are 108 bytes apart from Out1 through Out8.

fir_specific_ui_controls.source: user screenshots from Track DSP FIR408 GUI, 2026-05-06

highpass_frequency.label: HighPass
highpass_frequency.ui_behavior_reported: fixed while Type is BYPASS; editable when an active FIR filter Type is selected

visible_or_inferred_range.note: Sequence inferred from visible dropdown screenshots; verify exact full list by capture or GUI inspection.

notes: The screenshots show the complete visible FIR-specific control group: Mode, HighPass, LowPass, Type, Win, Taps, and OK.
Visible tap list includes 604 in the screenshot; this may be a GUI font rendering of 608. Treat exact tap enum values as open until captured.

gain.note: Inherited from DspLib-408.json for static config decode of common DSP408 gain fields.

peq_frequency.note: Inherited from the decoded DSP408 PEQ/crossover frequency model and confirmed by FIR 0x4B HighPass/LowPass raw samples.

fir_tap_count.note: Observed raw 8 -> 512 and raw 9 -> 544 in FIR generator 0x4B writes.

frame.length_semantics: payload byte at offset 2 counts command byte plus argument bytes

checksum.note: Same framing/checksum model as the existing DSP408 proxy decoder; all frames in the first FIR408 log were valid.

0x10.name: handshake_init
notes: FIR408 startup ack byte observed as 0x33.

value_models.highpass: u16le raw using the same exponential 19.7Hz..20.16kHz / 0..300 mapping as DSP408 crossover/PEQ frequency
value_models.lowpass: u16le raw using the same exponential 19.7Hz..20.16kHz / 0..300 mapping as DSP408 crossover/PEQ frequency

observed_examples.note: OK did not produce a separate non-meter command in this capture; the only observed write was the duplicated 0x4B generator state.

post_apply_cmd56_probe.result: all 12 observed 0x56 chunks remained FF-filled after the 0x4B apply capture

0x4C.name: fir_processing_mode_write

channel_mapping.predicted_outputs: Out1..Out8 use channel bytes 0x04..0x0B; endpoints Out1 and Out8 are observed, intermediate outputs are predicted from the standard contiguous DSP408 channel map.

0x4C.readback_observation: After setting Out1 to FIR, a full 0x27/0x24 config readback of blocks 0x00..0x1F matched the initial baseline byte-for-byte. This mode is therefore not proven to be stored in the normal config dump.

observed_external_impulse_upload.selector_current_best_meaning: InA/input-slot-0 after user clarified txt/csv import is available only for InA..InD

two_tap_sample.meaning: coefficient[0]=1.0, coefficient[1]=0.5; confirms float32 big-endian upload encoding

0x4F.name: external_fir_transfer_control
0x4F.observed_context: first non-meter command before the 0x4E external FIR coefficient chunk burst

0x56.name: fir_file_or_coefficient_chunk_read
0x56.observed_response_data_content: all 0xFF in first startup log; after external impulse upload, offset 0 returned little-endian float32 1.0 followed by zeros; after two-tap upload, offset 0 returned little-endian float32 1.0, 0.5, then zeros
0x56.hypothesis: Likely reads FIR file/coefficient or FIR display/state data for one selector or slot. The observed address/position sequence is not simple 52-byte paging, so the field may be a sample position, frequency bin, or FIR memory coordinate rather than a raw byte offset. The first log has no non-0xFF data bytes, so value encoding is not yet decoded.

0x5B.name: external_fir_name_write
0x5B.observed_context: last non-meter command after the 0x4E external FIR coefficient chunk burst

write.channel_map_status: Out1 and Out8 endpoints observed; Out2..Out7 predicted as 0x05..0x0A from contiguous DSP408 output map

fir_window_function.likely_scope: per FIR channel or per generated FIR design

fir_tap_count.likely_scope: per FIR channel or per generated FIR design

fir_highpass_frequency.ui_behavior_reported: fixed while Type is BYPASS; editable when an active FIR filter Type is selected
fir_highpass_frequency.likely_scope: per FIR channel or per generated FIR design

fir_lowpass_frequency.likely_scope: per FIR channel or per generated FIR design

fir_ok_apply.likely_meaning: apply generated FIR settings or selected external FIR settings

write.observation: Clicking OK after changing Out1 HighPass to 19.70 kHz produced only the duplicated 0x4B generator-state write; no distinct OK/apply command was captured.

manual_reference.note: PDF text extraction confirms an input-channel FIR import section. It also mentions External FIR in an output-channel section, but the current live GUI report says txt/csv import is only available on InA..InD.

selector_interpretation.current_best_meaning: InA/input FIR slot 0; InB/input FIR slot 1 is also observed
selector_interpretation.reason: User clarified that txt/csv External FIR import is only available for InA..InD in the live software; command selector 0x00 also matches the standard InA channel byte.

write_sequence_observed.meaning: Selector sample: InB repeated the same sequence with selector 0x01 in 0x4F, all 0x4E chunks, and 0x5B

readback_via_cmd56.offset_0_meaning: coefficient/display value 1.0 little-endian float32 followed by zeros
readback_via_cmd56.two_tap_offset_0_meaning: coefficient/display values 1.0 and 0.5 as little-endian float32
readback_via_cmd56.sequential_observation: offsets 0, 13 and 26 returned valid zero-filled float32 data after the first impulse value; offset 39 returned partial zeros then FF; offsets 52 and above returned FF
readback_via_cmd56.readback_encoding: IEEE-754 float32 little-endian where data is present

open_questions: Confirm selector mapping endpoints by repeating the External FIR upload for InD; InA and InB are already observed.
Why sequential 0x56 reads after the impulse upload expose only the first approximately 47 float32 values before FF-filled responses.
Whether the output-section External FIR reference in the PDF is exposed in another software mode or hidden in the current GUI.

script_verification.result: confirmed 31 full 50-byte data blocks plus short block 0x1F with 6 data bytes

output_record_static_decode.note: The static layout decodes fields present in the 0x27/0x24 config dump. The live 0x4C IIR/FIR mode write was not observed to change this config dump.

notes: The 12 FIR file-name slots are contiguous 8-byte entries.
The final Out8File entry crosses from block 0x1E into the short block 0x1F.

content_after_external_impulse_upload.meaning: little-endian float32 1.0 followed by little-endian float32 zeros
content_after_external_impulse_upload.sequential_probe: offsets 0, 13, 26 returned valid 52-byte data; offset 39 returned 32 zero bytes then FF; offsets 52 and above returned FF

content_after_external_two_tap_upload.meaning: little-endian float32 1.0 followed by little-endian float32 0.5; confirms cmd56 readback follows coefficient order at low offsets

script_verification.executed_at_local: 2026-05-06T13:40:09 and rerun after external FIR upload at 2026-05-06T14:45:19

sequential_script_verification.result: selector 0x00 returned little-endian float32 coefficient/display data at low offsets after external impulse upload, then FF-filled responses from offset 52 upward

next_evidence_needed: Repeat External FIR upload with center impulse to identify how far cmd56 readback exposes the 512-tap array.
Capture 0x56 reads for selectors other than 0x00, if the GUI can trigger them.

open_questions.question: Does the 0x56 selector byte address input channel InA..InD directly, or an input FIR slot/bank?
Are the 52 response bytes FIR coefficients, file content, transfer status, or a directory/chunk payload?
Which write command changes one channel's FIR file assignment/name table entry?
Where are FIR enable and bypass fields stored, separately from the now observed Out1 IIR/FIR mode write?

tooling_notes.note: The general readBlock() helper in the current Java toolchain is limited to 0x00..0x1C, but the first FIR408 startup log observed config reads through 0x1F. FIR scripts therefore use direct write("00 01 02 27 XX", 0x24) requests for blocks 0x1D..0x1F until the toolchain is deliberately generalized.

Scripts that inject read/probe payloads should establish a fresh proxy-side session with connect(); resetSession(); ensureSession(); handshake(); before sending active reads. Attaching to a stale GUI-ended session can log PC_TO_DSP frames without receiving DSP_TO_PC responses.
